refnamediv.refpurpose: Update one option of the current object and returns an updated object.

listitem.para: The current Random Number object. At output, the object is updated with the new settings.
The name of the option to configure. The key can have one of the following values :
"-dimension" : configures the dimension of the space, i.e. the size of the vector returned by the command lowdisc_next
"-method" : the random number generator to use (default : "scilabrand").

The following sequences are available :
"vandercorput" : the Van Der Corput low discrepancy sequence which is based on a script
"halton" : the Halton low discrepancy sequence which is based on a script
"faure" : the Faure low discrepancy sequence which is based on a script
"-seedmethod" : the type of computation for the seed (default : "constant"). This feature is not available yet.

"-vandercorputbasis" : configures the basis used in the Van Der Corput sequence (default : 2). The value is expected to be a positive integer, greater or equal to 2. It should be a prime number, but that is not checked by the configure method.

"-primeslist" : configures the matrix of prime numbers used in the Halton sequence. The expected matrix must have a 1xn shape, with n an integer greater than 2. The default list is made of the 100 first prime numbers, from 2 to 541. This list of primes is used in the Halton sequence. That default list should enable the user to generate sequences in dimension up to 100. If a larger dimension problem is to process, that feature should enable users to customize the list to meet the required dimension. The user should be warned that the Halton sequence may produce poor convergence rate if the dimension is larger than 15.

"-sequenceindex" : the starting index of the low discrepancy sequences. When a Random Number object is created this setting is initialized to 0. Whenever the randonumber_next method is called, the sequence index is updated and incremented with 1. If this option is passed to the lowdisc_cget method, it allows to know the number of vectors which have allready been generated. If this option is set, it allows to modify the behaviour of the low discrepancy sequences, which take into account this index to produce the next element in the sequence. The -sequenceindex should therefore generally not be set, except in specific situations where the user has particular requirements on the low discrepancy sequence to generate. Whatever the value of the -sequenceindex option, it has no effect on the values produced by the pseudo-random number generators.

refsection.title: Description
refsection.para: This command allows to configure the state of the current Random Number object. It requires to take the current object both as an input and an output argument.

refsection.title: Examples
refsection.para: In the following example, one creates a new random number generator and configure it so that a Faure sequence is setup with the value 1 as the seed.

refsection.title: Example : how to extend the list of primes used in Halton sequence
refsection.para: In the following example, one creates a new Halton sequence generator and configure the list of primes with a 200 primes table. These primes are then used to generate a sequence up to the 30-th term. That feature is available because the Halton sequence is a Scilab script. The gslhalton sequence, based on the GSL library does not possess such a feature (but may be faster, because it is based on a compiled C source code).
